title: cota tinctoria subsp. australis
intro: Cota tinctoria subsp. australis, also known as Australian weld or dyer's weed, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Australia and has been used for centuries for its medicinal and dye-making properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cota tinctoria subsp. australis include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be used as a natural dye for fabrics and textiles.
